interactions.summary: The closing panels offer forecasts. Sam expects weakening and eventual people-led leadership; Sara expects a non-agreement agreement, Iran turning to China and Russia, and no regime change. Talabani denies stealing U.S. weapons and argues for a larger Middle East deal, then rejects decapitation-regime-change logic because bombing causes national rallying.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Piers Morgan and guests; diarization is UNKNOWN throughout, attribution inferred from introductions and direct address.

speaker_notes.note: Machine speaker labels are all UNKNOWN; speaker turns are inferred from host questions, named introductions, and direct address rather than diarization.

claims.claim: Sam predicts the Iranian regime will weaken over years and create an opportunity for leadership by and from the people.
Sara predicts a non-agreement agreement with toll-like compensation, uneasy Israel relations, Iran moving toward China and Russia, and no regime change.
Talabani says no weapons were stolen on his Suleymaniyah side and calls any theft from allies shameful if it occurred elsewhere.
Talabani argues the U.S. should pursue a larger Middle East deal involving Palestinians and Lebanon rather than a narrow immediate-conflict win.
Talabani says bombing has adverse effects: people know who is bombing schools and hospitals, and people cannot be bombed into loving the attacker.

signature_moments.moment: Sara forecasts a wounded but strengthened Iran turning outward for the next round.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Iran will almost certainly run to China and Russia and try to beef up its defenses for the next round
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This phrase carries the packet argument in compact public-readable form.

signature_moments.moment: Talabani gives the moral core of the anti-regime-change argument.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you can’t bomb people into loving you
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This phrase carries the packet argument in compact public-readable form.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Sara’s forecast for a face-saving settlement where Trump exits while side deals compensate Iran.
Talabani’s proposed wider settlement addressing Palestine, Lebanon, and conflict drivers beyond Iran alone.

chronology_notes.note: Predictions in this packet distinguish six-month uncertainty from Sam’s three-to-five-year change horizon and Sara’s immediate postwar settlement forecast.
